$comment: Schema for the recursive signing tool's config file. Each config defines certificates (with ESRP signing operations) and rules that map files to those certificates.

certificates.$comment: List of certificate definitions. Each entry defines a friendly name, its ESRP signing operations, and optional flags.

friendlyName.$comment: Unique name used to reference this certificate in the rules section. Case-insensitive matching.

alwaysSign.$comment: When true, files matching this certificate are signed even if they already carry a signature. Used for dual-signing scenarios (e.g. adding a Microsoft signature on top of a third-party signature).

operations.$comment: ESRP signing operations submitted for files matching this certificate. Each operation is passed directly to the ESRP CLI submission JSON.

keyCode.$comment: ESRP key code identifying the signing certificate (e.g. 'CP-230012').

parameters.$comment: Operation parameters. May be an object with key/value pairs or an array of {parameterName, parameterValue} objects, depending on the ESRP operation.

rules.$comment: Rules that map files to certificate friendly names. Files are matched by exact filename first, then by extension.

fileNameMappings.$comment: Maps exact file names (case-insensitive) to certificate friendly names. Checked before extension mappings.

fileExtensionMappings.$comment: Maps file extensions (e.g. '.dll', '.exe') to certificate friendly names. Extensions should include the leading dot. Case-insensitive.
